_meta.description: Crypto algorithm detection patterns for JS VM opcode analysis
_meta.note: Patterns match computational structure, not specific opcode numbers. Use opcode_behavior (shift/xor/add/and/or/not/rot/load/store/cmp/jmp) for matching. User can extend by adding new entries.
references: FoC: Figure out Cryptographic Functions (2024) — 47 primitives

XTEA.description: XTEA block cipher (Extended Tiny Encryption Algorithm)
XTEA.notes: Common in Chinese anti-bot (TDC, Tencent ecosystem). Round: (v0<<4 ^ v0>>5) + v0 ^ sum + key[sum&3]

TEA.notes: Predecessor of XTEA. Used in QQ protocol and some game anti-cheat.

XXTEA.notes: Variable-length block cipher. MX = (z>>5^y<<2) + (y>>3^z<<4) ^ (sum^y) + (key[(p&3)^e]^z). Used in some JS obfuscators.

MD2.notes: 18 rounds of S-box substitution (256-byte permutation table derived from pi). Extremely rare in modern JS but exists in legacy systems.

MD4.notes: 3 rounds x 16 operations. Predecessor of MD5. Constants shared with SHA-1 K1/K2. Used in NTLM hashing.

MD5.notes: 4 rounds x 16 operations. F/G/H/I functions use AND/OR/XOR/NOT. Common in fingerprint hashing.

SHA1.notes: 80 rounds with 4 different functions. Still used in HMAC and some legacy systems.

SHA256.notes: 64 rounds. Sigma/sigma functions use rotate+shift+xor. Common in token generation.

SHA384.notes: Same algorithm as SHA-512 but different IV and truncated output. 64-bit words.

SHA512.notes: Same structure as SHA-256 but 64-bit words and 80 rounds.

SHA3_Keccak.notes: 24 rounds of theta/rho/pi/chi/iota. 5x5 state matrix of 64-bit lanes. RC constants are round-dependent. Detect by 24 distinct round constants.

BLAKE2b.notes: 12 rounds of G function (add-xor-rotate). IV same as SHA-512 fractional parts. Faster than SHA-256 on 64-bit.

BLAKE2s.notes: 10 rounds. 32-bit version of BLAKE2b. IV same as SHA-256. Rotation amounts: 16,12,8,7.

BLAKE3.notes: 7 rounds (reduced from BLAKE2s). Same G function. Tree structure for parallelism. IV = SHA-256 IV.

HMAC.notes: ipad=0x36, opad=0x5C. Wraps any hash function. Detect by XOR with these constants.

AES.notes: SBox lookup (256-byte table starting with 0x63,0x7C,0x77,0x7B). 10/12/14 rounds for 128/192/256-bit keys.

RC4.notes: KSA: 256 iterations of swap(S[i], S[j]). PRGA: continuous swap+XOR. Detect by 256-iteration init loop + swap pattern.

RC5.notes: Data-dependent rotations. P=0xB7E15163 (odd(e-2)*2^32), Q=0x9E3779B9 (golden ratio). Key expansion uses P+i*Q.

RC6.notes: Based on RC5 with multiplication. Same P/Q constants. 4 working registers (A,B,C,D).

ChaCha20.notes: Quarter-round: a+=b; d^=a; d<<<16; c+=d; b^=c; b<<<12; a+=b; d^=a; d<<<8; c+=d; b^=c; b<<<7. Constants = 'expand 32-byte k'.

Salsa20.notes: Similar to ChaCha20 but different quarter-round structure. Same constants.

DES.notes: 16 rounds Feistel. 8 S-boxes (6-bit to 4-bit). Detect by permutation tables or S-box constants.

Blowfish.notes: 16 rounds Feistel with key-dependent S-boxes. P-array initialized from pi digits.

Twofish.notes: 16 rounds Feistel with key-dependent S-boxes + MDS matrix. PHT (Pseudo-Hadamard Transform) + 1-bit rotation.

Serpent.notes: 32 rounds SPN. 8 different 4-bit S-boxes applied bitslice. Key schedule uses golden ratio. Conservative design (most rounds of AES finalists).

Camellia.notes: Feistel with FL/FL^-1 layers. Sigma constants derived from SHA-256 IV. Japanese/EU standard (CRYPTREC/NESSIE).

SEED.notes: 16 rounds modified Feistel. Uses golden ratio for key schedule (same as TEA). Two 8-bit S-boxes (SS0, SS1).

CAST128.notes: 12 or 16 rounds Feistel. Three types of round function (add/xor/sub). 4 S-boxes (256 entries each). Used in PGP.

GOST_28147.notes: 32 rounds Feistel. 8 different 4-bit S-boxes (implementation-defined). 11-bit left rotation. Russian standard.

Tiger.notes: 3 passes x 8 rounds. 4 S-boxes (256 entries of 64-bit). Designed for 64-bit platforms. Used in some P2P protocols.

Whirlpool.notes: AES-like structure (SubBytes/ShiftColumns/MixRows/AddRoundKey) on 8x8 state. 10 rounds. ISO/IEC 10118-3.

RIPEMD160.notes: Dual-path Merkle-Damgard. 80 rounds (5 groups of 16). Used in Bitcoin address generation.

CRC32.notes: Per-byte: 8 iterations of shift-right + conditional XOR with polynomial 0xEDB88320. Very common in data integrity checks.

Adler32.notes: s1 = (s1 + byte) % 65521; s2 = (s2 + s1) % 65521. Faster than CRC32 but weaker. Used in zlib/deflate.

PBKDF2.notes: Iterative HMAC. Detect by high iteration count of HMAC calls + XOR accumulation.

Base64.notes: 6-bit extraction + table lookup. Detect by AND 0x3F + 64-char lookup table. btoa/atob in JS.

XOR_Cipher.notes: Single-byte or multi-byte XOR. Very common in JS obfuscation for string decryption. Detect by repeated XOR in a loop.

SM3.notes: Chinese national standard. Similar structure to SHA-256. Used in Chinese government/finance systems.

SM4.notes: Chinese national standard. 32 rounds with non-linear transformation (S-box) + linear transformation (circular shift + XOR).

MurmurHash3.notes: Finalization: h ^= h >> 16; h *= 0x85EBCA6B; h ^= h >> 13; h *= 0xC2B2AE35; h ^= h >> 16. Very common in JS hash tables and fingerprinting.

FNV1a.notes: Per-byte: hash ^= byte; hash *= FNV_prime. Extremely simple. Common in JS string hashing. 32-bit: offset=0x811C9DC5, prime=0x01000193.

xxHash32.notes: Accumulator: acc = (acc + input * PRIME2) <<< 13 * PRIME1. Finalization uses multiply-shift-multiply. Very fast.

SipHash.notes: 2 compression rounds + 4 finalization rounds. ARX structure. Constants = 'somepseudorandomlygeneratedbytes'. Used in hash table DoS protection.

IDEA.notes: 8.5 rounds. Operations: XOR, addition mod 2^16, multiplication mod 2^16+1. NO fixed constants (tables computed from key at runtime). Detect by structure only: interleaved mul/add/xor on 16-bit words.

Poly1305.notes: Polynomial evaluation mod 2^130-5. Clamping mask on r: clear bits 4,8,12..124 and top 4 bits of each 32-bit word. Often paired with ChaCha20 (RFC 8439).

HKDF.notes: Two phases: Extract (HMAC(salt, IKM)) and Expand (iterative HMAC). Detect by two sequential HMAC calls with output of first as key of second.

HAVAL.notes: 3/4/5 passes of 32 rounds each. Uses Blowfish P-array constants for message expansion. 32 boolean functions. Variable output length.

SAFER.description: SAFER (Secure And Fast Encryption Routine) K-64/SK-128
SAFER.notes: SPN with byte-level operations. Uses exp(x, 45) and log(x, 45) mod 257 as S-boxes. Key-dependent rotation. Massey design.

Skipjack.notes: 32 rounds unbalanced Feistel. Two rule types (A and B) alternating. Single 256-byte F-table (permutation). 80-bit key. No public constants (F-table is the signature).

WAKE.notes: Stream cipher using 256-entry T-table. Key-dependent table generation. Detect by 256-entry table access pattern + XOR/shift combination.

MARS.notes: Heterogeneous structure: 8 forward mixing + 8 keyed transformation + 8 backward mixing. Uses multiplication for diffusion. Two 512-entry S-boxes.
